flashcards.Back: JavaScript Object Notation. A lightweight, text-based data interchange format that is easy for humans to read and write and easy for machines to parse and generate.
A collection of key-value pairs wrapped in curly braces { }. it is the JSON equivalent of a Python dictionary or an associative array.
An ordered list of values wrapped in square brackets [ ]. it is the JSON equivalent of a Python list or a standard programming array.
A string wrapped in double quotes that acts as a label for a piece of data. it appears before the colon in a key-value pair.
The data associated with a key. it can be a string, number, boolean, null, object, or array, and appears after the colon.
The fundamental building block of a JSON object, consisting of a quoted key, a colon, and a valid JSON value.
The specific syntax character used to separate a key from its corresponding value within a JSON object.
The character used to separate multiple items in an array or multiple key-value pairs in an object. it is not used after the final item.
The characters used to define the boundaries of a JSON object.
The characters used to define the boundaries of a JSON array.
A sequence of zero or more Unicode characters wrapped in double quotes (e.g., "hostname"). Single quotes are not valid in standard JSON.
A data type representing a numerical value (integer or floating-point). Unlike strings, numbers are not wrapped in quotes (e.g., 1024).
A data type representing one of two logical values: true or false. These are written in lowercase without quotes.
A special value representing the intentional absence of any value. it is written as 'null' without quotes.
The practice of placing objects inside other objects or arrays inside objects to represent complex, hierarchical data structures.
Standard JSON requires all keys and all string values to be wrapped in double quotes. using single quotes will result in a syntax error.
The spaces, tabs, and newlines in a JSON file. they are ignored by machines but are used by humans to make the data more readable.
A category of text-based languages like JSON and XML used to represent and transmit data structures over a network.
A document that defines the required structure and data types for a JSON file, used to validate that the data is correct before processing.
The structured data returned by a REST API after a request. it is almost always formatted as a JSON object or array.
A characteristic of JSON arrays where the items maintain their specific sequence (0, 1, 2...). this is important for representing lists like interface names.
A characteristic of JSON objects where the sequence of key-value pairs does not technically matter, as long as the keys are unique.
The process performed by an automation script (like Python) to read a JSON string and convert it into native data structures for processing.
The standard MIME type used in the HTTP 'Content-Type' or 'Accept' headers to indicate that the data being sent or requested is in JSON format.
The process of removing all unnecessary whitespace from a JSON file to reduce its size for faster transmission over the network.
Special sequences (like \" or \n) used within a JSON string to represent characters that would otherwise break the syntax.
A property of JSON where the keys (labels) explain the meaning of the values, making the data easier to understand without external documentation.
The process of formatting minified JSON with proper indentation and line breaks to make it readable for human troubleshooters.
The single top-level object or array that contains all other data within a JSON file.
YANG is a data modeling language used to define structure, while JSON is one of several formats (along with XML) used to encode and transmit the actual data.
